name: Islamic Center of Southern California
mission: THE ISLAMIC CENTER OF SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA IS AN INDEPENDENT ORGANIZATION. ITS PURPOSE IS TO PRACTICE AND SHARE THE VALUES OF ISLAM IN THE UNITED STATES ...
theoryOfChange: By providing spiritual, educational, and recreational activities, the center develops a vibrant American Muslim identity and fosters community support.
scoreSummary: Islamic Center of Southern California shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: The Islamic Center's Zakat Program provides financial assistance to those in need on the first three Fridays of each month. The Zakat Committee was created to provide limited financial aid to assist community members who need temporary support. On their 'Donate' page, they list 'Zakat & Sadaqah – Fulfill your religious obligations and assist those in need within our community.' They also provide detailed information on 'Understanding Zakat al-Fitr, Zakat al-Maal, and Fidya' with instructions on how to pay online, in person, or by mail, specifying 'Zakat al-Fitr,' 'Zakat al-Mal,' or 'Fidya' in the memo. (Source: https://islamiccenter.com/support/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 92% of total expenses

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 6.7% of revenue ($141,000 on $2,120,052)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

strengths.detail: The organization operates with <cite id="1">zero fundraising expenses</cite>, ensuring that no donor funds are diverted to professional solicitors or marketing campaigns.
The center maintains <cite id="1">17.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Center of Southern California's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and financial resilience. With <cite id="3">total assets of $3,369,879</cite> and a <cite id="1">working capital ratio of 13.1 months</cite> of operating expenses, the organization is well-positioned to weather economic fluctuations. This stability supports a wide array of services, from <cite id="5">weekly lectures and Ramadan programs</cite> to <cite id="5">civic engagement and food pantry operations</cite>. Furthermore, the center's <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> ensures a high level of oversight and governance, which is critical for an organization of its scale and community importance.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Southern California (ICSC) serves as a prominent <cite id="4">independent organization dedicated to practicing and sharing Islamic values</cite> within the United States. Operating as a multifaceted community hub, the center reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,134,893 in 2023</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="3">$2,111,995 in public contributions</cite>. Its programmatic reach extends beyond religious services to include <cite id="5">Friday Jumu’ah, Ramadan Iftar sponsorship, and a community food pantry</cite>. While the organization maintains a long-standing presence in the region, its financial profile shows a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 49.6%</cite>, which is notably lower than the <cite id="1">peer program ratio median of 85.3%</cite> for similar religious and cultural institutions. Despite this, the center demonstrates exceptional <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency, spending $0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in donations. The ICSC also provides essential social infrastructure through <cite id="5">interfaith engagement, funeral services, and counseling</cite>, positioning itself as a vital resource for the Southern California Muslim community.
